persona.name: Alex Chen
persona.role: Product Manager at a B2B SaaS startup
persona.core_preferences: Concise and direct. Professional but not stiff. Clear call to action. Short paragraphs.
persona.hidden_preferences: Information should follow a strict emotional arc: acknowledge effort/situation FIRST (1 sentence max), then deliver the news, then explain WHY it matters (stakes, not just facts), then one concrete next step — violating this order even if all elements are present feels wrong. The 'why' framing should always be from the READER's perspective, not the sender's — 'This matters because your team planned around this date' not 'This matters because we want quality'; drafts that center the sender's reasoning feel tone-deaf. Closing lines must be forward-looking and action-oriented — no 'thanks for your patience' or motivational platitudes; trailing sentiment after the CTA weakens the email. When multiple people need to do different things, prefers ONE clear owner with a deadline over group asks — 'Everyone please review' is a non-action — but also hates over-specified action items that assume org structure details the email shouldn't contain.
persona.dealbreakers: Corporate jargon, passive aggressive tone, walls of text, excessive exclamation marks
persona.initial_task: Help me draft an email to our engineering team about pushing the product launch date by two weeks due to a critical bug we found in testing.
